text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check individual pieces of code to ensure they work correctly. Integration tests go further, verifying that different parts of the system work together smoothly. Together, these practices build confidence in the software. A popular approach is test-driven development, where you write tests before writing the code itself. This ensures every new feature has tests from the start. When tests pass, developers can be more confident that changes won't break existing functionality. Ultimately, testing saves time by catching problems early and makes software more reliable for users.
